text: Attackers bypass EDR API hooking in user mode by performing {{c1::direct system calls}} (Syscalls) to transition to kernel mode directly, bypassing NTDLL.
explanation: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) systems hook standard Windows API functions in user-mode `ntdll.dll` to monitor process behavior. By loading the raw assembly instructions for syscalls directly, malware can transition to kernel mode without executing hooked functions.
usage: Tools like Syswhispers are used to generate custom assembly headers and dynamic syscall stubs for evasion.
spanish: Los atacantes evaden los ganchos de la API de EDR en modo de usuario realizando llamadas directas al sistema (Syscalls) para pasar al modo de núcleo directamente, evadiendo NTDLL.
